channel.title: MiSTerZine
channel.description: Short, source-checked tidbits on MiSTer FPGA releases, written by AI from cited sources and verified before publishing.

item.title: Night Slashers
item.description:
The Data East board behind Night Slashers guarded its main processor with an "Encrypted DECO156 ARM CPU," and rmonic79's new MiSTer FPGA core, which landed in the public database yesterday, reproduces it "with runtime on-board decrypt." So rather than shipping a pre-decrypted program, the FPGA reimplementation undoes the chip's encryption live as the hardware runs.

MiSTer debut 2026-07-10. Sources: Wikipedia, core repo.

item.title: Trio The Punch - Never Forget Me...
item.description:
Trio The Punch, whose Data East arcade core reached the public MiSTer FPGA database on 2026-07-08, did not start out as a beat 'em up at all. Per Wikipedia, it was "initially planned as a sugoroku game," but "the content was completely changed during production." What shipped was a surreal brawler across 35 levels, published by Data East and released only in Japan.

MiSTer debut 2026-07-08. Sources: Wikipedia, core repo.

item.title: Battle Garegga
item.description:
In arcade shmup Battle Garegga, thirty years old this February, powering up your weapon and grabbing items pushes the difficulty higher, while dying drops it. The catch: the fewer lives you hold when you die, the more the difficulty falls. Players are "forced" to keep themselves powered down, conserve shots, and lose lives on purpose to keep the final stages playable.

30th anniversary; MiSTer debut 2023-09-10. Sources: Wikipedia.

item.title: Cabal
item.description:
Cabal, the 1988 TAD Corporation arcade game now on MiSTer, put the player character in the foreground under an over-the-shoulder camera, "similar to modern third-person shooters." Its success spawned a wave of clones, including NAM-1975 and Wild Guns.

MiSTer debut 2026-07-05. Sources: Wikipedia.

item.title: Biomechanical Toy
item.description:
From its 1995 arcade cabinet, Biomechanical Toy waited decades for a home version: its first console appearance came only via the Gaelco Arcade 1 compilation for the Evercade. Now on MiSTer, the Gaelco/Zeus run and gun is playable outside an arcade board by a second route entirely.

MiSTer debut 2026-07-05. Sources: Wikipedia.

item.title: Flash Boy
item.description:
Flash Boy (Data East, 1981) is now on MiSTer, a title for the DECO Cassette System. Its games shipped on standard Microcassette tapes, and inserting one copied the program into the cabinet's RAM chips, a process that took about two to three minutes before you could play.

MiSTer debut 2026-06-30. Sources: Wikipedia.

item.title: Darius II (Dual Screen)
item.description:
The MiSTer core for Darius II runs the dual-screen version. Taito's game shipped in a three-screen configuration like the original Darius too, but "while a three-screen version like the original exists, Darius II is more commonly and generally represented as a two-screen game." Outside Japan it went by another name entirely: Sagaia.

MiSTer debut 2026-06-16. Sources: Wikipedia.
